verdict_reasoning: B2M (beta-2-microglobulin) is the non-covalently associated light chain of MHC class I heterodimers. It lacks a transmembrane domain of its own but is co-assembled in the ER with MHC class I heavy chain (HLA-A/B/C) and co-trafficked to the cell surface on virtually all nucleated cells. On the plasma membrane it forms a stable, constitutive complex with the MHC class I heavy chain and peptide, sitting on the extracellular face. Abundant flow cytometry, surface biotinylation, and anti-B2M antibody staining on intact non-permeabilized cells confirm surface display. Multiple therapeutic programs (e.g., anti-B2M antibodies, bispecifics in hematologic malignancies) target this surface pool. This fits the 'stable_complex_partner' category: no membrane anchor of its own, but assembled intracellularly with an anchored surface protein and co-displayed constitutively.
